signals.headline: Trump Confirmed Kevin Warsh as Powell's Fed Successor in January; Warsh Testified Before Senate Banking Committee on Apr
signals.raw:
Trump Confirmed Kevin Warsh as Powell's Fed Successor in January; Warsh Testified Before Senate Banking Committee on April 21, 2026** | **NO** | **HIGH** confidence[1][2][3][4][6]

No new confirmations, announcements, or resolutions have occurred since the April 21 Senate hearing to alter prediction markets on Powell's replacement—Warsh's nomination (announced January by Trump) requires Senate Ban
